stories.title: Flanagan Park getting new playground, ADA path with $750K federal grant

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Flanagan Park in central Colorado Springs is getting a major upgrade — new playground, ADA-accessible walking path, lighting, and a resurfaced basketball court, funded by a $750K federal grant.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Community input shaped the Flanagan Park renovation in Colorado Springs.

New playground + ADA access + lighting = a neighborhood park that finally works for everyone.

Project is underway. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 8:00 AM MT

Flanagan Park in central Colorado Springs is getting its biggest upgrade in decades — and it was shaped by the people who use it most.

The City of Colorado Springs secured a $750,000 federal community development grant to renovate the park at 3132 N. Prospect Street. The project includes a brand-new playground, an ADA-accessible walking path with pedestrian lighting and benches, new screen fencing, a resurfaced basketball court, and improvements to the existing landscaping, irrigation system, and picnic pavilion.

Flanagan Park was originally built in the mid-1970s and serves a diverse, multi-generational neighborhood that includes families, older adults, and people in assisted living. The city collected community input over several years to shape the project priorities — and the results reflect what residents actually asked for.

This is the kind of infrastructure investment that makes a neighborhood park genuinely useful for everyone, especially families with young children and people who need accessible paths to enjoy the space.

When it's done, Flanagan Park will be one of the most complete neighborhood parks in central Colorado Springs.

What improvements would make YOUR neighborhood park better to visit?

---

article.headline: Flanagan Park Is Getting Its First Major Renovation in Decades — Here's What's Coming

COS families with young children — PAW Patrol Live is coming to Pikes Peak Center this weekend.

PAW Patrol Live "Heroes Unite" plays at 190 S. Cascade Ave in downtown Colorado Springs on:

Saturday, May 23: 10:00 AM and 2:00 PM

In the show, Chase, Marshall, Skye, and the Mighty Pups have to use their Mighty Pup Powers to stop a supervillain from ruining Adventure City — cue the teamwork, the action, and the very excited children in the audience.

Tickets are available now at AXS.com or in person at the Pikes Peak Center box office. This is the kind of live show that young children talk about for months afterward — and a great way to kick off the summer with something special.

Have you taken your children to a live show at Pikes Peak Center before?

article.headline: PAW Patrol Live Is in Colorado Springs This Weekend — Everything Families Need to Know

stories.title: Amazon baby bath seat recalls — nearly 9,000 units pose drowning risk

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
SAFETY ALERT: Multiple baby bath seats sold on Amazon have been recalled due to tip-over and drowning risk.

Brands: YCXXKJ, NFSVLB, CheerKid, Trankerloop. Nearly 9,000 units total.

Stop using. Full refund at CPSC.gov. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Check your Amazon order history.

CPSC has recalled 4 brands of baby bath seats — all due to tip-over risk and drowning hazard. If you have one: stop use immediately and go to CPSC.gov for a full refund.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 12:00 PM MT

If you have a baby bath seat that was purchased on Amazon, please check this carefully.

The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) has recalled multiple brands of infant bath seats due to a tip-over risk that poses a risk of serious injury or death by drowning. The recalled brands include:

- YCXXKJ (sold on Amazon by BenTalk)
- NFSVLB (sold on Amazon by NFSVLB)
- CheerKid (sold on Amazon by Babibaby and Woot)
- Trankerloop (recalled February 26, 2026; approximately 2,380 units)

Combined, nearly 9,000 units are affected across these recalls.

The hazard: the bath seats can tip over while in use. A baby who is placed in a bath seat assumes it will hold them securely. If the seat tips, they can slip into the water immediately.

If you have any of these products, stop using them now. Contact the seller through Amazon or go directly to CPSC.gov to report the product and request a full refund. You can also call the CPSC hotline at 800-638-2772.

Please share this with any parents or grandparents who may have purchased infant bath seats on Amazon recently.

---

article.headline: Baby Bath Seat Recall: 4 Amazon Brands Pulled for Drowning Risk — Check Your Bathroom Now

stories.title: COS Trackchair program returns with expanded accessible hikes

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
COS Trackchair Program is back — free guided hikes at Ute Valley, Red Rock Canyon, Blodgett, and Bluestem Prairie.

Two hikes daily, Wed–Sun. Reserve now at coloradosprings.gov.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 3:00 PM MT

Colorado Springs has one of the best outdoor recreation programs in the state — and it just got better for families and individuals with mobility challenges.

The City of Colorado Springs is bringing back its Trackchair Program for 2026 with an expanded schedule: two guided hikes daily, Wednesday through Sunday, at rotating locations throughout the season.

The locations include:
- Ute Valley Park
- Red Rock Canyon Open Space
- Blodgett Open Space
- Bluestem Prairie Open Space

All reservations are free. You can book your spot now through the City of Colorado Springs parks program.

The Trackchair is an all-terrain motorized chair that allows people who use wheelchairs or have limited mobility to access trails that would otherwise be inaccessible. For families who have a member with a physical disability, this program opens up the outdoor experiences that Colorado Springs is known for.

If you or someone in your family has been waiting for an accessible way to experience COS trails, this is the summer to do it.

Have you used the Trackchair Program before? We'd love to hear about your experience.

---

article.headline: Free Accessible Hiking Is Back in Colorado Springs — COS Trackchair Program Expands for 2026

stories.title: D11 last day is May 22 — what COS families need this week

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
D11 families: last day of school is THIS Friday, May 22.

Summer is officially here. PPLD Summer Adventure is open for registration. Paw Patrol Live is this weekend. Trackchair hikes are free. Get the list: ppld.org

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Window: 5:00 PM MT

Colorado Springs District 11 families — the last day of school is this Friday, May 22.

Summer 2026 starts this week, and there is plenty happening in COS to make the most of it from day one.

A few things to know:

PPLD Summer Adventure "Unearth a Story" is live for registration now at ppld.org. The free program runs all summer with activities, milestones, and rewards for readers of all ages.

PAW Patrol Live is at Pikes Peak Center THIS WEEKEND — May 23 and 24. Tickets at AXS.com.

The COS Trackchair Program is offering free accessible hikes at Ute Valley, Red Rock Canyon, Blodgett, and Bluestem Prairie on Wednesdays through Sundays. Families with members who have mobility challenges can reserve a spot now.

Flanagan Park is in the middle of a major renovation, but there are dozens of other neighborhood parks across COS ready for summer use.

However families celebrate the end of the school year, Colorado Springs has plenty of ways to fill the next three months.

How is your family planning to spend the first weekend of summer?

---

article.headline: D11 Last Day Is May 22: Your COS Family Summer Launch Guide
